items.title: Arsenal edge past 10-man Chelsea, stretch PL lead
items.summary: Arsenal have gone five points clear at the top of the Premier League table again after edging past Chelsea in a tense London derby on Sunday.

items.title: Sesko nets another winner in United comeback
items.summary: Manchester United came from a goal down to claim another morale-boosting victory over Crystal Palace on Sunday.

items.title: Pulisic's Milan score late double, 10 pts off Inter
items.summary: AC Milan needed two late goals to secure a 2-0 win at Cremonese in Serie A on Sunday and bounce back from its second loss of the season.

items.title: Qatar postpones all games; no news on Finalissima
items.summary: The Qatar football federation postponed all tournaments and matches until further notice on Sunday amid global tensions following the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran.

items.title: 'Als Twente zo speelt, kunnen ze het Feyenoord en Ajax lastig gaan maken'
items.summary: FC Twente boekte een belangrijke zege op Feyenoord in de eigen Grolsch Veste en durft daardoor weer naar boven te kijken. Clubwatcher Geert-Jan Jakobs gaf na afloop zijn analyse van de wedstrijd.

items.title: Timber held van Arsenal dankzij specialiteit van het huis
items.summary: Jurriën Timber heeft Arsenal zondagavond aan de zege geholpen in de kraker tegen Chelsea. De Nederlander kopte uit een corner binnen en bezorgde zijn ploeg daarmee een vliegende start in een cruciale maand.

items.title: VI Live: Antony held van Real Betis na omhaal in El Gran Derbi
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op de rol. Ajax en Feyenoord hebben een apart liveblog. Hier volg je onder meer de ontmoeting tussen FC Utrecht en AZ. Arsenal ontvangt Chelsea en in Italië staan AS Roma en Juventus tegenover elkaar. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Arsenal 2-1 Chelsea: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Scott 4 min: A fairly shapeless start, actually. “The best pints are Thursday night.” Hello Ian McCourt, formerly of this parish. “Or about 1.30pm on a Friday.” 2 min: A mid-octane start to the game. No real shape yet, though it’s obvious that Palmer is prowling the left wing today, opposite to his usual beat. Continue reading...

items.title: Players covering mouths during confrontations should be sent off - Infantino
items.summary: Players who cover their mouths when speaking to opponents during confrontations should be sent off, says Gianni Infantino.

items.title: Feyenoord-huurling Zerrouki brengt ode aan Twente-fans: 'Nergens beter dan hier'
items.summary: Nergens is de sfeer beter dan in Enschede, aldus Ramiz Zerrouki. De door Feyenoord aan FC Twente verhuurde middenvelder genoot zondagmiddag van de sfeer in de Grolsch Veste.

items.title: Van Persie laat veelzeggende statistiek links liggen: 'Laat ik aan jullie'
items.summary: Feyenoord leed zondag uit bij FC Twente de zevende competitienederlaag van het seizoen. Een verslaggever merkte op dat de Rotterdammers dit seizoen slechts twee punten hebben gepakt tegen clubs uit de topzes van de Eredivisie, maar daar kan Van Persie weinig mee.

items.title: Timber converts ANOTHER Arsenal corner to regain lead
items.summary: Timber converts ANOTHER Arsenal corner to regain lead against Chelsea in the Premier League.

items.title: What now for 'epic' Scottish title race after Old Firm draw?
items.summary: Amid one of the most captivating title fights in the history Scotland's top flight, BBC Scotland looks at the state of play for each of the four clubs involved.

items.title: Iran, the US, and a World Cup that starts in three months
items.summary: What does the US conflict with Iran mean for what was already set to be a highly politicised World Cup this summer?

items.title: Tudor: Fulham opener 'ridiculous' as he accuses Jimenez of 'cheating'
items.summary: Tottenham boss Igor Tudor has criticised the officials' "ridiculous" decision to allow Fulham's opener in their 2-1 loss at Craven Cottage as he accused Raul Jimenez of "cheating" for Harry Wilson's goal.

items.title: Carrick 'in the box seat' for Man Utd job as his team take a 'big step'
items.summary: Gary Neville's pre-match claim that Michael Carrick was in the box seat for the Manchester United job looked to have backfired when Maxence Lacroix headed Crystal Palace in front after four minutes at Old Trafford.

items.title: 'Hearts & Motherwell the winners after damaging Old Firm stalemate'
items.summary: BBC Scotland's chief sportswriter Tom English evaluates the impact of Sunday's draw at Ibrox.

items.title: Sesko streak continues as Manchester United fight off 10-man Crystal Palace
items.summary: As the second half began, a banner appeared in the Stretford End that read: “MUFC proudly colonised by immigrants”. If this was a riposte to Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s assertion that these shores have been overrun by those from overseas (for which the co-owner half-heartedly ­apologised), Manchester United needed their own reply to a listless opening period that left them trailing to Maxence ­Lacroix’s early header. Eleven ­minutes into the restart, they did. Continue reading...

items.title: Wilson and Iwobi earn Fulham win to increase Tottenham relegation fears
items.summary: As each passing week goes by, the threat of relegation becomes more real for Tottenham. This is no longer a quirk that it can be assumed will automatically be corrected. The monster not only exists but is getting closer, and fear is beginning to set in and make itself seen in their play. Defeat on Sunday was not only far more emphatic than the scoreline might suggest, but it was their fourth reverse in a row, extending their run without a win in the Premier League to nine. The only sliver of relief was that all their relegation rivals lost as well. But this was grim stuff from Spurs. Igor Tudor had hoped the 4-1 defeat in the north London derby would be a wake-up call. But if anything, this was even worse. Arsenal won because they are better than Tottenham; Fulham won because Spurs were terrible. Tudor, the arch-firefighter, has a huge job on his hands. Continue reading...

items.title: Welbeck lifts Brighton to pile pressure on Nottingham Forest and Vítor Pereira
items.summary: These are concerning times for Vítor Pereira. Nottingham Forest’s fourth permanent manager of this crazy season may be only four matches into his spell in charge but – given the record of the owner, Evangelos Marinakis – his position is already looking most precarious. A second Premier League defeat in succession since he was appointed to replace Sean Dyche a fortnight ago leaves Forest only two points clear of the drop zone and facing a daunting trip to Manchester City on Wednesday. Continue reading...
